# llama-index-memory-unison

> Python LlamaIndex memory provider backed by the Unison brain — a drop-in
> replacement for `ChatMemoryBuffer` that gives your agents persistent,
> knowledge-graph-powered long-term recall across sessions.

**Agents: start with [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)** — it tells you how to install,
authenticate with `UNISON_TOKEN`, and use `UnisonMemory.from_client(session_id=...)`
as a drop-in for `ChatMemoryBuffer`.

## Docs
- [README.md](README.md): install, quickstart, env vars, how it works, releasing
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how an agent installs, authenticates, and integrates UnisonMemory
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): build, test, PR checklist
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): vulnerability reporting

## Usage
- Install: `pip install llama-index-memory-unison`
- Auth: `export UNISON_TOKEN=usk_...`
- Drop-in: `UnisonMemory.from_client(session_id="user-123", api_key="usk_...")`
- From env: `UnisonMemory.from_defaults(session_id="user-123")`

## Examples
- [README.md — Quick start](README.md#quick-start): ReActAgent with UnisonMemory
- [README.md — Replacing ChatMemoryBuffer](README.md#replacing-chatmemorybuffer): before/after
- [src/llama_index/memory/unison/](src/llama_index/memory/unison/): source — `base.py` (UnisonMemory), `_client.py` (HTTP client)
